{"key_event":"Liberalization amid executive-dominated multiparty rule","reasoning":"Multiparty politics, independent media, churches, NGOs, and constitutional activism kept civil society relatively strong, but job losses, weakened unions, opposition harassment, and emergency powers pulled it below the 1990 transition peak. State power declined as privatization, copper-sector weakness, debt, and donor conditionality reduced fiscal and economic command, even though the Chiluba presidency retained meaningful coercive and patronage capacity.","society_change":-0.3,"society_power":6.7,"state_change":-0.6,"state_power":6.1}
